sections.heading: Right to information
sections.body_text: Subject to the provisions of this Act, all citizens shall have the right to information.

sections.heading: Obligations of public authorities
sections.body_text: Every public authority shall maintain records duly catalogued and indexed, publish certain categories of information, and proactively disclose information to the public to promote transparency and accountability.

sections.heading: Application for obtaining information
sections.body_text: A person who desires to obtain any information under this Act shall make a request in writing or electronically to the Public Information Officer, specifying the particulars of the information sought.

sections.heading: Disposal of request
sections.body_text: The Public Information Officer shall provide the information within 30 days of the receipt of the request. If the information concerns the life or liberty of a person, it shall be provided within 48 hours.

sections.heading: Exemptions from disclosure of information
sections.body_text: Certain categories of information are exempt from disclosure, including information prejudicial to the sovereignty and integrity of India, information forbidden by court, trade secrets, and personal information without public interest.

sections.heading: Severability
sections.body_text: If a request for information is partially exempt, access may still be provided to the non-exempt portions, provided they can be reasonably severed from the exempt portions.

sections.heading: Third party information
sections.body_text: If the information sought pertains to a third party and is treated as confidential, the Public Information Officer shall give the third party an opportunity to make a representation before disclosing the information.

sections.heading: Constitution of Central Information Commission
sections.body_text: The Central Government shall constitute a Central Information Commission to exercise the powers and perform the functions assigned under this Act.

sections.heading: Term of office and conditions of service of Chief Information Commissioner and Information Commissioners
sections.body_text: The Chief Information Commissioner and Information Commissioners shall hold office for a term of three years or until they attain the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.

sections.heading: Removal of Chief Information Commissioner or Information Commissioners
sections.body_text: The Chief Information Commissioner or an Information Commissioner may be removed from office only by an order of the President on grounds of proved misbehavior or incapacity.

sections.heading: Constitution of State Information Commission
sections.body_text: Every State Government shall constitute a State Information Commission to exercise the powers and perform the functions assigned under this Act.

sections.heading: Term of office and conditions of service of State Chief Information Commissioner and State Information Commissioners
sections.body_text: The State Chief Information Commissioner and State Information Commissioners shall hold office for a term of three years or until they attain the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.

sections.heading: Removal of State Chief Information Commissioner or State Information Commissioners
sections.body_text: The State Chief Information Commissioner or a State Information Commissioner may be removed from office only by an order of the Governor on grounds of proved misbehavior or incapacity.

sections.heading: Appeals
sections.body_text: Any person who does not receive a decision within the specified time or is aggrieved by a decision of the Public Information Officer may file an appeal to the first appellate authority and subsequently to the Central or State Information Commission.

sections.heading: Penalties
sections.body_text: If the Public Information Officer fails to provide information without reasonable cause, a penalty of Rs. 250 per day may be imposed, subject to a maximum of Rs. 25,000.

sections.heading: Protection for whistleblowers
sections.body_text: The Act provides protection to individuals who disclose information in good faith regarding corruption or wrongdoing in public authorities, ensuring they are not victimized for their actions.
